title: Volkswagen put 100 sheep to work under 31,000 solar panels
summary: Volkswagen has traded lawnmowers for sheep at the massive solar farm that helps power one of its factories in Poland, and the animals are doing more than just keeping the grass short.

title: Kia’s electric hatch gains AWD and nearly 350 miles of range
summary: Kia launched the new all-wheel drive (AWD) EV4 variant in Europe, but it’s not quite the range-topping GT model.

title: What I saw inside Lectron’s EV charger factory in China
summary: Whether you know the Lectron name or not, there’s a good chance you’ve used one of its EV charging adapters. The company makes many of the J1772/CCS-to-NACS and NACS-to-J1772/CCS adapters that EV drivers rely on. Now, Lectron has launched the NEXUS, its second-gen Level 2 home EV charger. I recently toured Lectron’s factory in China to see how the company has become a go-to supplier for both EV drivers and automakers like Ford, GM, and Mercedes-Benz.

title: Sunrun wants to pay you to turn your home into an AI data center
summary: Sunrun wants to turn thousands of solar-powered homes into a giant distributed AI data center – and pay homeowners to help power the AI boom.

title: Ford admits its $30K midsize EV pickup is actually ‘small’
summary: Ford’s midsize EV pickup, due out next year, will be smaller than expected. For the first time, Ford referred to it as an “all-new, affordable small electric pickup” built on the UEV platform.

title: China is bringing buttons back to cars, ending trend led by Tesla
summary: China will require automakers to include physical buttons for safety-related functions, pushing back on the proliferation of using screens for everything in modern cars.

title: Lectric XP4 750 e-bike gets FREE $498 flash sale cargo bundle, Navee Summer EV Sale, EGO Select Cut + Touch Drive mower $599, more
summary:
At the front of our midweek Green Deals pack is a cargo-focused flash sale on Lectric’s XP4 750 Folding Utility e-bike with a $498 FREE bundle at $1,299. Coming up behind it is Navee’s Summer Sale lineup of EV deals, like the UT5 Max Electric SuperScooter at its $1,300 low, as well as EGO’s advanced 21-inch Cordless Electric Select Cut + Touch Drive Lawn Mower kit at a $599 2026 low, the meross Wi-Fi 2-Zone Water Timer for Garden Hoses at $88, a returning low price on Govee’s Matter Outdoor Lamp Post – plus, some solid camping-ready deals and soon-to-end holiday sales. As always, you can also find the latest hangover deals down at the bottom of the page, like yesterday’s Anker SOLIX Summer Power Sale exclusive power station deals, the returning $299 low on ECOVACS’ Ultramarine P1 cordless robotic pool cleaner, and more.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: Xiaomi makes Sky Nomad official: new EREV brand to take on Li Auto
summary:
Xiaomi officially confirmed “Sky Nomad,” the name of its new extended-range electric vehicle (EREV) effort, after months of rumors. The first model is a full-size SUV expected to deliver over 1,500 km (932 miles) of combined range.

The announcement puts China’s hottest EV newcomer on a collision course with Li Auto and Huawei-backed Aito, which dominate the country’s lucrative family SUV segment.

title: This $22K EV coupe has a semi-solid-state battery and a ‘Queen’ makeup station in China
summary: The MG 07 is the electric coupe we want, but can’t have. It’s about to go on sale in China with a semi-solid-state EV battery offering nearly 400 miles (610 km) of range, the industry’s first “Queen” makeup station, and a bunch of other premium extras for about $22,000.

title: BYD’s first pickup goes on sale for $63,000 in the UK, with more EV range than the Ford Ranger
summary: BYD launched the Shark, its first pickup in the UK and across Europe, priced from about $63,000, with 56 miles of pure-electric range. Powered by its Super Hybrid plug-in powertrain, BYD claims the Shark “brings performance far in advance of what can be achieved by traditional diesel pickups.”

title: You Think Your Electric Lawnmower is Eco Friendly? Volkswagen Just Replaced Its Lawnmowers With Sheep
summary: Volkswagen replaced the mechanical lawnmowers at its factory in Poland with a flock of 100 sheep.

title: BYD's PHEV Pickup Just Landed In The U.K. But Americans Still Can't Have It
summary: The Shark pickup was already on sale in China, Mexico, South America, and Australia, leaving the U.S. as the odd one out.

title: The EU Wants To Use Satellites To Automatically Prevent Your Car From Speeding: Report
summary: If the satellite-based detection system realizes your car is speeding, regulators want it to automatically reduce your car's power output.

title: Another Chinese EV Player Just Landed In North America
summary: Leapmotor launched its B10 crossover in Mexico, just as the U.S. is cracking down harder than ever on Chinese cars.

title: Fiat's $13,995 EV For America Is Cute, But Barely A Car
summary: With a top speed of 19 mph and range of 46 miles, the Fiat Topolino isn't even street legal without an optional pack to raise its top speed.

title: The Alpine A110 EV Has One Job: Prove Electric Sports Cars Can Be Light
summary: The A110 Future mule previews Alpine’s plan to make an EV sports car that still feels small, balanced, and genuinely driver-focused.

title: Mercedes' New Production Plan Could Make The 'Baby' G-Class EV Far Cheaper
summary: Mercedes’ smaller G-Class may not be built in Germany but in Hungary alongside the brand’s other budget models.

title: Skip The Rivian R2 Waitlist For Just $20,000 Over Sticker Price
summary: The first used Rivian R2 has hit the market with just 50 miles on the odometer and a price tag that's $19,000 above MSRP.

title: After EV Batteries, China Wants To Dominate Car Brains Too
summary: Several Chinese carmakers are developing their own smart-driving chips, aiming to cut costs, move faster, and rely less on third parties.

title: This 2023 Tesla Model 3 Has The 'Durable' Battery. It Still Degraded
summary: Its LFP pack had 90% health at 26,000 miles. That’s not a disaster, but it’s worse than the chemistry’s reputation suggests.
